description: Executable evals for the glossary skill (per docs/superpowers/decisions/2026-05-15-glossary-skill.md §4). Each eval shells `claude --print`, captures the response, and runs rubric assertions against it. Scenarios cover: lazy ./CONTEXT.md creation on first term, _Avoid_ alias recording, format compliance with grill-with-docs CONTEXT-FORMAT.md, DTP/SA caller-hook offer-on-resolution and skip-on-no-resolution, decline-does-not-write, and Flagged-ambiguity surfacing on conflicting alias. Per memory note `feedback_sunk_cost_eval.md`: substrate-fixable failures stay at the eval-runner layer; do NOT chase them with skill or rules edits.
_contract_note: Required-tier `tool_input_matches` assertions pin the Claude Code Skill tool surface (tool name 'Skill', input_key 'skill', input_value 'glossary'). This is an unversioned upstream contract owned by Claude Code. If an upstream rename lands, every required-tier assertion in this file silently flips red without signaling platform change vs. regression. Confirm Skill tool schema before treating a required-tier failure as a behavioral regression.

evals.name: lazy-create-on-first-term
evals.summary: First /glossary invocation in a project with no existing ./CONTEXT.md must lazy-create the file with the grill-with-docs format (heading + Language section).
evals.prompt:
/glossary Customer

This project has no ./CONTEXT.md yet. Project context name: "Reflex Ordering". Description: "Order intake and fulfillment for the Reflex retail platform." Definition of Customer: "A person or organization that places orders." Aliases to avoid: client, buyer, account. No relationships to record.

assertions.description: /glossary slash command invokes the glossary skill
File body contains the project-context heading
File body contains the ## Language section header
Customer is recorded as a bolded canonical term
Aliases recorded under _Avoid_:

evals.name: alias-of-recorded-under-canonical
evals.summary: /glossary <alias> --alias-of <canonical> records the alias on the _Avoid_: line under the canonical term, not as a separate entry.
evals.prompt:
Project ./CONTEXT.md already exists with this content:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment for the Reflex retail platform.

## Language

**Customer**:
A person or organization that places orders.
_Avoid_:
```

Now run: /glossary account --alias-of Customer

assertions.description: /glossary invokes glossary skill
account appears on the _Avoid_: line
account is NOT recorded as a separate canonical term

evals.name: format-matches-grill-context-format
evals.summary: Output structure matches grill-with-docs CONTEXT-FORMAT.md: heading, description, ## Language section with bold-term + definition + _Avoid_ pattern.
evals.prompt:
/glossary Order

Project context: "Reflex Ordering". Description: "Order intake and fulfillment." Definition of Order: "A request from a Customer to purchase one or more items." No aliases. No relationships.

assertions.description: Has a top-level heading line
Has ## Language section
Bold-term name on its own line followed by definition (grill format)

evals.name: dtp-hook-offers-on-resolution
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation from define-the-problem with a resolved candidate term must produce a /glossary offer to the user.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a define-the-problem session. During the five questions the user disambiguated the word "account" — it should canonically be "Customer". The Problem Statement now uses "Customer". ./CONTEXT.md does not yet have a Customer entry.

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract: /glossary --offer-from-caller=define-the-problem --candidate-terms=Customer

assertions.description: Caller-hook invokes glossary skill
Surfaces an offer to canonicalize Customer in ./CONTEXT.md
Names the candidate term in the offer

evals.name: dtp-hook-skips-when-no-resolution
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation with no candidate terms (no resolution occurred) must NOT offer canonicalization.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a define-the-problem session. The five questions did not surface any new domain terms; the Problem Statement uses only generic vocabulary. ./CONTEXT.md does not exist.

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract with no candidates: /glossary --offer-from-caller=define-the-problem --candidate-terms=

assertions.description: Does NOT offer canonicalization when no candidates passed

evals.name: sa-hook-offers-on-named-system
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation from systems-analysis with a recurring named service candidate must produce a /glossary offer.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a systems-analysis dependency mapping. The user named a service "Sentinel" as a shared component that recurred 3× in the dependency summary. ./CONTEXT.md does not yet have a Sentinel entry.

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract: /glossary --offer-from-caller=systems-analysis --candidate-terms=Sentinel

assertions.description: Caller-hook invokes glossary skill
Surfaces a canonicalization offer for Sentinel
Names the candidate service in the offer

evals.name: sa-hook-skips-on-no-new-system
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation where every candidate is already in ./CONTEXT.md must NOT re-offer.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a systems-analysis dependency mapping. The summary mentions "Sentinel" — but ./CONTEXT.md ALREADY contains:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment.

## Language

**Sentinel**:
Fraud-detection service that gates order placement.
_Avoid_:
```

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract: /glossary --offer-from-caller=systems-analysis --candidate-terms=Sentinel

assertions.description: Does NOT re-offer Sentinel; it is already canonicalized

evals.name: decline-does-not-write
evals.summary: When the user declines a hook offer, no file write occurs and ./CONTEXT.md content is unchanged.
evals.prompt:
Caller-hook invocation: /glossary --offer-from-caller=define-the-problem --candidate-terms=Customer

When you offer to canonicalize Customer, I will reply: "no". After my decline, do NOT write to ./CONTEXT.md. ./CONTEXT.md does not currently exist.

assertions.description: No Write or Edit tool call after the user declines the offer

evals.name: flagged-ambiguity-on-conflict
evals.summary: When --alias-of targets a term already used as canonical for a different concept, the conflict is recorded under Flagged ambiguities.
evals.prompt:
Project ./CONTEXT.md content:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment.

## Language

**User**:
An authenticated person who can place orders or browse the catalog.
_Avoid_:

**Customer**:
A person or organization that places orders.
_Avoid_:
```

Now run: /glossary User --alias-of Customer

(User is currently a distinct canonical entry; aliasing it to Customer is a conflict.)

assertions.description: Surfaces the conflict (Flagged ambiguities section, or warning before writing)
Asks for explicit user confirmation before destructive rewrite
